title: Steven Spielberg Argued With THIS UFO Researcher About How Aliens Should Be Portrayed
summary: This interview discusses Jacques Vallée, a preeminent UFO researcher who inspired Steven Spielberg's character in Close Encounters of the Third Kind. The conversation covers Vallée's disagreement with Spielberg about how aliens should be portrayed, with Vallée favoring a time travel hypothesis over the extraterrestrial hypothesis.

acronyms.definition: An aerial phenomenon that cannot be immediately identified

key_claims.claim: Jacques Vallée was Steven Spielberg's inspiration for the eccentric French scientist played by François Truffaut in Close Encounters of the Third Kind
Jacques Vallée argued with Steven Spielberg on the set of Close Encounters about how UFOs should be portrayed
Jacques Vallée wanted UFOs and aliens in the movie to be linked with time travel, not just space travel
The extraterrestrial hypothesis is more far-fetched than alternatives
Jacques Vallée is obsessed with time travel

topics: Close Encounters of the Third Kind
